03 — NBC News Article Data Cleaning & EDA¶

Exploratory analysis of scraped NBC article data. This notebook investigates data completeness, NA patterns, and data quality issues to inform downstream cleaning decisions.

Datasets:

  • nbc_scraped_all.csv — scraped NBC News articles

3.1. Imports & Data Loading¶

In [24]:
# Put all imports here
import pandas as pd
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
import requests
import re
In [4]:
# Load data here
# fox = pd.read_csv("../data/raw/fox_scraped_all.csv")
nbc = pd.read_csv("../data/raw/nbc_scraped_all.csv")
all_urls = pd.read_csv("../data/external/url_only_data.csv")
In [5]:
# Check that nbc scraper returned all links that it should have
len(all_urls[all_urls["url"].str.contains("nbcnews.com")]) == len(nbc)
Out[5]:
True

3.2 NBC Dataset — NA Exploration¶

Investigating missing values across title, author, and datetime_posted columns to understand data quality issues and plan cleaning strategies.

In [6]:
# List NA values by column
nbc.isna().sum()
Out[6]:
url                 0
topic               0
title               4
subtitle            4
author             96
datetime_posted    94
label               0
dtype: int64
In [7]:
# Start with title column (smallest NA count & same as subtitle)
nbc[nbc["title"].isna()]
Out[7]:
url topic title subtitle author datetime_posted label
323 https://www.nbcnews.com/select/shopping/select... select NaN NaN NaN NaN NBC News
354 https://www.nbcnews.com/feature/nbc-out/lil-na... feature NaN NaN NaN NaN NBC News
613 https://www.nbcnews.com/select/shopping/new-no... select NaN NaN NaN NaN NBC News
1786 https://www.nbcnews.com/select/shopping/mr-cof... select NaN NaN NaN NaN NBC News
In [8]:
# Examine actual links manually
broken_urls = nbc[nbc["title"].isna()]["url"].values # All 4 NAs are pages that don't load. -> title and subtitle are both 4 NA so they are the same 4 articles
print(f"Articles with missing titles: {len(broken_urls)}")
print("Conclusion: all are pages that fail to load")
broken_urls

Articles with missing titles: 4
Conclusion: all are pages that fail to load

In [ ]:
# Look at articles with NAs in author that don't share URLs with the title NAs
nbc[~(nbc["title"].isna()) & (nbc["author"].isna())]["url"].values #visual inspection seems to indicate that these are all live updates

In [ ]:
# Check if author NAs (excluding title NAs) are all live updates
print("Only 80 of the 92 articles with NA author are live updates, the next step is to examine the remaining 12.")
print("The below 80 articles can be handled together with one script that extracts all named contributors using meta tags in <head> into a comma separated list \nthat we can decide what to do with that later.")
nbc[~(nbc["title"].isna()) & (nbc["author"].isna()) & (nbc["url"].str.contains("live-updates"))] # Only 80 of 92 are live-updates, need to inspect remaining 12 
# -> these 80 can be handled together with one script
# Can extract all named contributors using meta tags in <head> into a comma separated list and then decide what to do with that later

Only 80 of the 92 articles with NA author are live updates, the next step is to examine the remaining 12.
The below 80 articles can be handled together with one script that extracts all named contributors using meta tags in <head> into a comma separated list 
that we can decide what to do with that later.
url topic title subtitle author datetime_posted label

In [ ]:
# Inspect remaining 12 urls that have NA author, non-NA title, and are not live updates
print("The remaining 12 URLs all seem to be different and will need to be handled individually.")
nbc[~(nbc["title"].isna()) & (nbc["author"].isna()) & ~(nbc["url"].str.contains("live-updates"))]["url"].values # These all seem to be different, will need to be handled individually

The remaining 12 URLs all seem to be different and will need to be handled individually.

In [ ]:
# We know there are 80 live updates, so look at remaining 10 aritcles with datetime_posted NA
nbc[~(nbc["title"].isna()) & (nbc["datetime_posted"].isna()) & ~(nbc["url"].str.contains("live-updates"))]

In [ ]:
# Verify: do the 10 non-live-update datetime NA articles overlap with the 12 non-live-update author NA articles?
dt_na_urls = set(nbc[~(nbc["title"].isna()) & (nbc["datetime_posted"].isna()) & ~(nbc["url"].str.contains("live-updates"))]["url"])
author_na_urls = set(nbc[~(nbc["title"].isna()) & (nbc["author"].isna()) & ~(nbc["url"].str.contains("live-updates"))]["url"])

exclusive_to_dt_na = dt_na_urls - author_na_urls
print(f"URLs with datetime NA but not author NA: {len(exclusive_to_dt_na)}")  # Expect 0

URLs with datetime NA but not author NA: 0

3.3. Summary of NA Value Exploration for NBC Dataset¶

Key Findings:¶

Title NAs (4 articles):

  • All 4 articles with missing titles are pages that don't load
  • Both title and subtitle are NA for the same 4 articles

Author NAs (92 articles):

  • 80 of 92 articles with missing authors are live-update articles
  • These 80 can be handled uniformly by extracting named contributors from meta tags in HTML <head> as comma-separated lists
  • Remaining 12 articles with NA authors (non-live-update, non-NA title) are diverse and will need individual handling

Datetime_posted NAs (90 articles):

  • All 80 live-update articles have missing datetime_posted values
  • Remaining 10 articles with missing datetime_posted (non-live-update, non-NA title) overlap completely with the 12 articles that have NA author values
  • These articles will need individual investigation

Data Quality Recommendations:¶

  1. Remove or flag the 4 articles with missing titles (pages that don't load)
  2. Handle the 80 live-update articles uniformly using meta tag extraction
  3. Individually process the 12 remaining problematic articles that have both missing author and datetime_posted values

3.4 NBC Data Cleaning¶

3.4.1 Get rid of all 4 title/subtitle NAs¶

This section gets rid of the 4 articles that have broken links.

In [11]:
nbc_01 = nbc[~(nbc["title"].isna()) & ~(nbc["subtitle"].isna())]

3.4.2 Handle live update article pages¶

This section creates a function that extracts the relevant fields from the live update-style blogs.

In [17]:
# Get all articles with live blog format
nbc[~(nbc["title"].isna()) & (nbc["author"].isna()) & (nbc["url"].str.contains("live-updates"))].url.values
Out[17]:

  }

    font-family: 'PublicoText';

</style><style>
  @layer vars {
    :root {
      --red-1: #FFB4B4;
      --red-2: #FD6F6F;
      --red-3: #FA3A3A;
      --red-4: #E91717;
      --red-5: #CB1111;
      --red-6: #AE0E0E;
      --red-7: #911010;
      --blue-1: #DBEBFF;
      --blue-2: #82AEFF;
      --blue-3: #5081FF;
      --blue-4: #3061FF; /* (P) */
      --blue-5: #2051D1;
      --blue-6: #1B4698;
      --blue-7: #102039; /* (P) */
      --brand-blue: var(--blue-7);
      --gray-1: #F2F2F2;
      --gray-2: #EBEBEB;
      --gray-3: #CCCCCC;
      --gray-4: #999999;
      --gray-5: #555555;
      --gray-6: #2A2A2A;
      --neutral-gray-1: #F2F2F2;
      --black: #000;
      --bg-1: #FFF;
      --bg-2: var(--gray-1);
      --bg-3: var(--gray-6);
      --btn-bg: var(--blue-4);
      --btn-txt: #FFF;
      --error: var(--red-5);
      --focus: var(--gray-1);
      --line: var(--blue-4);
      --link: var(--blue-4);
      --link-hover: var(--blue-2);
      --link-underline-color: transparent;
      --link-hover-underline-color: transparent;
      --text-black: var(--gray-6);
      --text-white: #FFF;
      --text-0: var(--text-black);
      --text-1: var(--gray-1);
      --text-2: var(--gray-2);
      --text-3: var(--gray-3);
      --text-4: var(--gray-4);
      --text-5: var(--gray-5);
      --font-mono: "FoundersGroteskMono", monospace;
      --font-sans: "FoundersGroteskCond", "Arial Narrow", sans-serif;
      --font-sans-cond: var(--font-sans);
      --font-serif: "PublicoText", serif;
      --font-display-serif: "PublicoHeadline", serif;
      --font-display-sans: var(--font-sans);
      --font-heading: var(--font-display-sans);
      --font-body: var(--font-serif);
      --font-weight-body: normal;
      --font-weight-heading: 500;
      --font-space-after-body: 1.25rem;
      --body-font-size: 16px;
      --font-size-0: 0.875rem; /* 14px */
      --font-size-1: 1rem; /* 16px */
      --font-size-2: 1.125rem; /* 18px */
      --font-size-3: 1.25rem; /* 20px */
      --font-size-4: 1.5rem; /* 24px */
      --font-size-5: 2rem; /* 32px */
      --font-size-6: 2.25rem; /* 36px */
      --graf-font-size-1: var(--font-size-1);
      --graf-font-size-2: var(--font-size-2);
      --hfs-mobile-height: 60px;
      --hfs-desktop-height: var(--hfs-mobile-height);
      --hfs-global-list-height: 0;
      --sticky-ui-offset: 20px;
      --elections-nav-height: 40px;
      --topic-subnav-height: 48px;
      --list-marker-color: var(--blue-4);
      --list-marker-gutter: 0;
      --ordered-list-marker-gutter: 0.8rem;
      --ordered-list-marker-size: 1.5rem;
      --ordered-list-marker-color: var(--list-marker-color);
    }
  }

president'","url":"https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2024-election/live-blog/dnc-2024-live-updates-rcna165226#rcrd52655","datePublished":"2024-08-20T04:23:14.765Z","dateModified":"2024-08-20T04:28:30.249Z","author":[{"@type":"Person","name":"Megan Lebowitz","image":"https://media-cldnry.s-nbcnews.com/image/upload/t_fit-1500w,q_auto:best/newscms/2024_26/3653107/240627-megan-lebowitz.jpg","jobTitle":"Politics Reporter","description":"Megan Lebowitz is a politics reporter for NBC News.","disambiguatingDescription":"I joined the politics digital team in 2023 after completing an NBC News rotational program, where I worked on the assignment desk and coordinated live shots for MSNBC and NBC News Now. My digital coverage focuses on breaking politics news, and I have also written enterprise and fact-checking stories. I graduated from Northwestern University, where I studied journalism and political science. A midwesterner at heart, I grew up outside of Cleveland.","url":"https://www.nbcnews.com/author/megan-lebowitz-ncpn1275337","sameAs":["https://x.com/megan_lebowitz"]},{"@type":"Person","name":"Christopher Cicchiello","description":"Christopher serving president and his young age when he was elected senator. \n\nBiden said he has been \"too young to be in the Senate because I wasn't 30 yet and too old to stay as president. \n\n\"I can honestly say Iâm more optimistic about the future than I was when I was elected as a 29-year-old United States senator â I mean it,\" he added.","image":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://media-cldnry.s-nbcnews.com/image/upload/t_fit-1500w,q_auto:best/rockcms/2024-08/240816-dnc-liveblog-jg-d2a0f2.jpg","caption":""}},{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"BlogPosting","publisher":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"NBC our best presidents, she was also a vice president,\" Biden joked.\n\n\"That was a joke, that was a joke,\" he said.\n\nFifteen vice presidents, including Biden, have gone on to become president.","image":"https://media-cldnry.s-nbcnews.com/image/upload/mpx/2704722219/2024_08/1724129518179_now_joe_harris_dnc_240819_1920x1080-y8b8mg.jpg"},{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"BlogPosting","publisher":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"NBC warns that the threat seen on Jan. 6 is still 'very much alive'","url":"https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2024-election/live-blog/dnc-2024-live-updates-rcna165226#rcrd52651","datePublished":"2024-08-20T04:18:42.060Z","dateModified":"2024-08-20T04:23:44.825Z","author":[{"@type":"Person","name":"Rebecca Shabad","image":"https://media-cldnry.s-nbcnews.com/image/upload/t_fit-1500w,q_auto:best/newscms/2023_08/3595832/rebecca-shabad-byline-jm-1.jpg","jobTitle":"Politics Reporter","description":"Rebecca Shabad is a politics reporter for NBC News based in Washington.","disambiguatingDescription":"Before NBC News, I was a political reporter at CBS News, covering national politics and policy for its website during the 2016 presidential election cycle and the first year of Trumpâs presidency. I wrote about how Russian hackers could disrupt the U.S. election, issues surrounding voting machines and the Trump administrationâs vetting process for refugees. I also wrote an exclusive story that went viral about sleeveless dresses being banned in the House, which prompted a change in the dress code. At The Hill newspaper, I reported on the federal budget process, government spending as well as on breaking political news. I got my start in journalism at CQ Roll Call. Iâm a graduate of Syracuse Universityâs S.I. Newhouse School of Public Communications, with a degree in broadcast journalism and political science.","url":"https://www.nbcnews.com/author/rebecca-shabad-ncpn854016","sameAs":["https://x.com/rebeccashabad"]}],"articleBody":"Biden said this will be the first presidential election since Jan. 6, 2021. \n\n\"On that day, we almost lost everything about who we are as a country, and that threat, this is not hyperbole, that threat is still very much alive. Donald Trump says he will refuse to accept the election results if he loses again,\" Biden said. \n\nBiden said the country can't let Trump make the same attempt again.","image":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://media-cldnry.s-nbcnews.com/image/upload/t_fit-1500w,q_auto:best/rockcms/2024-08/240816-dnc-liveblog-jg-d2a0f2.jpg","caption":""}},{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"BlogPosting","publisher":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"NBC former national politics reporter for NBC News Digital.","url":"https://www.nbcnews.com/author/alex-seitz-wald-ncpn385646"}],"articleBody":"A group of pro-Palestinian protesters standing with the Minnesota delegation have turned their backs to Biden during his remarks. They are each holding a hand over their mouths. Seven protestersÂ are holding buttons that say ânot another bombâ and have âarms embargo nowâ written in red on their hands.Â \n\nAsma Mohammed, one of the uncommitted delegates from Minneapolis, said the plan was intentionally not to interrupt andÂ that they wanted to protest Biden. But they will prob not do same when Harris speaks because they feel likeÂ sheâsÂ moving in the right direction.","image":"https://media-cldnry.s-nbcnews.com/image/upload/rockcms/2024-08/IMG8593-a5fd27.jpg"},{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"BlogPosting","publisher":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"NBC talks up 'power of women' and bashes Trump's role in overturning Roe v. Wade","url":"https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2024-election/live-blog/dnc-2024-live-updates-rcna165226#rcrd52638","datePublished":"2024-08-20T04:16:11.530Z","dateModified":"2024-08-20T04:26:03.235Z","author":[{"@type":"Person","name":"ZoÃ« Richards","jobTitle":"Politics Reporter","description":"ZoÃ« Richards is a politics reporter for NBC News.","disambiguatingDescription":"I previously covered breaking news at The Daily Beast and Talking Points Memo.","url":"https://www.nbcnews.com/author/zoe-richards-ncpn1291151","sameAs":["https://x.com/zarichards"]}],"articleBody":"Biden touched on reproductive freedom, referring to Trump's role in appointing conservative Supreme Court justices who helped overturn the constitutional right to abortion.\n\nBiden cited the Supreme Court majority's writing in its decision to overturn Roe v. Wade that women \"are not without electoral or political power.â\n\n\"No kidding,\" Biden said. \"Republicans found out the power of women in 2022, and Donald Trump is going to find out the power of women in 2024.\"\n\nVoters are set toÂ weigh in onÂ constitutional amendmentsÂ to enshrine abortion rights in several states this fall.","image":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://media-cldnry.s-nbcnews.com/image/upload/t_fit-1500w,q_auto:best/rockcms/2024-08/240816-dnc-liveblog-jg-d2a0f2.jpg","caption":""}},{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"BlogPosting","publisher":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"NBC website during the 2016 presidential election cycle and the first year of Trumpâs presidency. I wrote about how Russian hackers could disrupt the U.S. election, issues surrounding voting machines and the Trump administrationâs vetting process for refugees. I also wrote an exclusive story that went viral about sleeveless dresses being banned in the House, which prompted a change in the dress code. At The Hill newspaper, I reported on the federal budget process, government spending as well as on breaking political news. I got my start in journalism at CQ Roll Call. Iâm a graduate of Syracuse Universityâs S.I. Newhouse School of Public Communications, with a degree in broadcast journalism and political science.","url":"https://www.nbcnews.com/author/rebecca-shabad-ncpn854016","sameAs":["https://x.com/rebeccashabad"]}],"articleBody":"Biden said his administration will keep working to bring hostages home and end the war in Gaza.Â \n\nâTwo days ago, I put forward a proposal that brought us closerâ to a cease-fire agreement âthan weâve done since Oct. 7,â he said.Â \n\nBiden said his administration, including Secretary of State Antony Blinken, is working to prevent a wider war, reunite hostages with their families and surge humanitarian health and food assistance into Gaza.Â \n\nHe also said of the anti-Israel demonstrators in Chicago: âThose protesters out in the street, they have a point. A lot of innocent people are being killed on both sides.â","image":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://media-cldnry.s-nbcnews.com/image/upload/t_fit-1500w,q_auto:best/rockcms/2024-08/240816-dnc-liveblog-jg-d2a0f2.jpg","caption":""}},{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"BlogPosting","publisher":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"NBC says he isn't angry at those who called for him to step aside","url":"https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2024-election/live-blog/dnc-2024-live-updates-rcna165226#rcrd52644","datePublished":"2024-08-20T04:15:29.331Z","dateModified":"2024-08-20T04:26:19.211Z","author":[{"@type":"Person","name":"Megan Lebowitz","image":"https://media-cldnry.s-nbcnews.com/image/upload/t_fit-1500w,q_auto:best/newscms/2024_26/3653107/240627-megan-lebowitz.jpg","jobTitle":"Politics Reporter","description":"Megan Lebowitz is a politics reporter for NBC News.","disambiguatingDescription":"I joined the politics digital team in 2023 after completing an NBC News rotational program, where I worked on the assignment desk and coordinated live shots for MSNBC and NBC News Now. My digital coverage focuses on breaking politics news, and I have also written enterprise and fact-checking stories. I graduated from Northwestern University, where I studied journalism and political science. A midwesterner at heart, I grew up outside of Cleveland.","url":"https://www.nbcnews.com/author/megan-lebowitz-ncpn1275337","sameAs":["https://x.com/megan_lebowitz"]}],"articleBody":"Biden addressed people who called on him to bow out of the presidential race, saying he was not angry.\n\n\"All this talk about how I'm angry at all those people who said I should step down, that's not true,\" he said.\n\nThe audience chanted, \"We love Joe.\"\n\n\"I love my country more, and we need to preserve our democracy,\" he said. officials forced to shorten opening night","url":"https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2024-election/live-blog/dnc-2024-live-updates-rcna165226#rcrd52643","datePublished":"2024-08-20T04:14:58.343Z","dateModified":"2024-08-20T04:26:58.518Z","author":[{"@type":"Person","name":"Nnamdi Egwuonwu","image":"https://media-cldnry.s-nbcnews.com/image/upload/t_fit-1500w,q_auto:best/newscms/2025_41/3707104/img_1017.jpg","description":"Nnamdi Egwuonwu is a reporter for MSNBC.","url":"https://www.msnbc.com/author/nnamdi-egwuonwu-ncpn1305655","sameAs":["https://x.com/nnamegwuon"]}],"articleBody":"Night one was almost too successful, convention officials said. The constant cheering and applause forced convention officials to skip planned speakers and performers to ensure Biden had enough time to complete his address, officials said in a statement \n\nâBecause of the raucous applause interrupting speaker after speaker, we ultimately skipped elements of our program to ensure we could get to President Biden as quickly as possible so that he could speak directly to the American people,\" the statement read.Â \"We are proud of the electric atmosphere in our convention hall and proud that our convention is showcasing the broad and diverse coalition behind the Harris-Walz ticket throughout the week on and off the stage.\"","image":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://media-cldnry.s-nbcnews.com/image/upload/t_fit-1500w,q_auto:best/rockcms/2024-08/240816-dnc-liveblog-jg-d2a0f2.jpg","caption":""}},{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"BlogPosting","publisher":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"NBC Grace Meng rescheduled as DNC Day 1 goes long","url":"https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2024-election/live-blog/dnc-2024-live-updates-rcna165226#rcrd52640","datePublished":"2024-08-20T04:04:54.780Z","dateModified":"2024-08-20T04:05:30.134Z","author":[{"@type":"Person","name":"Sahil Kapur","image":"https://media-cldnry.s-nbcnews.com/image/upload/t_fit-1500w,q_auto:best/newscms/2024_09/3640450/sahil_hs.jpg","jobTitle":"Senior National Political Reporter","description":"Sahil Kapur is a senior national political reporter for NBC News.","disambiguatingDescription":"I graduated with a B.A. in economics and government from Claremont McKenna College. In Washington, I got my start as a health care policy reporter for multiple publications and most recently worked at Bloomberg News, covering politics and a variety of policy issues. I've also appeared on CBS, ABC, Fox News, CNN, PBS and NPR to discuss my reporting.","url":"https://www.nbcnews.com/author/sahil-kapur-ncpn1123791","sameAs":["https://x.com/sahilkapur"]}],"articleBody":"Rep. Grace Meng, D-N.Y., was scheduled to speak at the convention tonight, but after it got off to a late start she never took the stage.\n\nShe âhas been rescheduled for another nightâ that is still to be announced, a spokesperson for Meng told NBC News.","image":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://media-cldnry.s-nbcnews.com/image/upload/t_fit-1500w,q_auto:best/rockcms/2024-08/240816-dnc-liveblog-jg-d2a0f2.jpg","caption":""}},{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"BlogPosting","publisher":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"NBC talks up jobs that don't require college degrees","url":"https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2024-election/live-blog/dnc-2024-live-updates-rcna165226#rcrd52553","datePublished":"2024-08-20T03:58:52.371Z","dateModified":"2024-08-20T04:02:55.839Z","author":[{"@type":"Person","name":"Megan Lebowitz","image":"https://media-cldnry.s-nbcnews.com/image/upload/t_fit-1500w,q_auto:best/newscms/2024_26/3653107/240627-megan-lebowitz.jpg","jobTitle":"Politics Reporter","description":"Megan Lebowitz is a politics reporter for NBC News.","disambiguatingDescription":"I joined the politics digital team in 2023 after completing an NBC News rotational program, where I worked on the assignment desk and coordinated live shots for MSNBC and NBC News Now. My digital coverage focuses on breaking politics news, and I have also written enterprise and fact-checking stories. I graduated from Northwestern University, where I studied journalism and political science. A midwesterner at heart, I grew up outside of Cleveland.","url":"https://www.nbcnews.com/author/megan-lebowitz-ncpn1275337","sameAs":["https://x.com/megan_lebowitz"]}],"articleBody":"Biden spent part of his speech promoting his support of jobs that do not require college degrees.\n\n\"We created apprenticeships with businesses and communities, putting students on a path to a good-paying job whether or not they go to college,\" he said. \"And by the way, we're making college a hell of a lot more affordable.\"","image":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://media-cldnry.s-nbcnews.com/image/upload/t_fit-1500w,q_auto:best/rockcms/2024-08/240816-dnc-liveblog-jg-d2a0f2.jpg","caption":""}},{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"BlogPosting","publisher":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"NBC says he's proud to be first president to walk a picket line","url":"https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2024-election/live-blog/dnc-2024-live-updates-rcna165226#rcrd52635","datePublished":"2024-08-20T03:56:02.598Z","dateModified":"2024-08-20T03:57:54.042Z","author":[{"@type":"Person","name":"Alicia Victoria Lozano","image":"https://media-cldnry.s-nbcnews.com/image/upload/t_fit-1500w,q_auto:best/newscms/2024_25/3652082/230203-alicia-lozano.jpg","jobTitle":"National Reporter","description":"Alicia Victoria Lozano is a California-based reporter for NBC News focusing on climate change, wildfires and the changing politics of drug laws.","disambiguatingDescription":"I helped lead coverage of the Lahaina fire in Maui and uncovered how a historic neighborhood became ground zero for the devastation. I have closely followed the challenges faced by wildland firefighters as they fight for better pay. And I was among the first reporters dispatched when gunmen opened fire in Uvalde, Texas; Monterey Park; Calif.; and Colorado Springs.Â Â ","award":"Mid-Atlantic Emmy - Interactivity, Preventing Suicide: Breaking the Silence, 2018","url":"https://www.nbcnews.com/author/alicia-victoria-lozano-ncpn1112366","sameAs":["https://x.com/aliciavlozano"]}],"articleBody":"Biden has often called himself the most pro-union president, and tonight he doubled down on that.\n\n\"Wall Street didnât build America,\" he said. \"Unions built the middle class.\"\n\nLast year, Biden joined striking members of the United Auto Workers near Detroit.\n\nUAW President Shawn Fain was among tonight's convention speakers, touting what he said is Biden's pro-worker agenda, saying Harris is the only candidate who will protect the right of workers to organize.","image":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://media-cldnry.s-nbcnews.com/image/upload/t_fit-1500w,q_auto:best/rockcms/2024-08/240816-dnc-liveblog-jg-d2a0f2.jpg","caption":""}},{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"BlogPosting","publisher":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"NBC senior reporter at NBC News.","disambiguatingDescription":"I have worked at NBC News for more than a decade. I have a masterâs degree from Columbia Journalism School.","url":"https://www.nbcnews.com/author/daniel-arkin-ncpn1206","sameAs":["https://x.com/d_arkin"]}],"articleBody":"In his remarks, Biden has repeatedly sought to tie Harris to the administration's top domestic agenda items, from infrastructure investments to lowering the cost of prescription drugs.\n\nWhen the crowd started chanting, \"Thank you, Joe,\" he jumped in with a suggested addition: \"Thank you, Kamala, too.\"","image":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://media-cldnry.s-nbcnews.com/image/upload/t_fit-1500w,q_auto:best/rockcms/2024-08/240816-dnc-liveblog-jg-d2a0f2.jpg","caption":""}},{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"BlogPosting","publisher":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"NBC says 'democracy has delivered, and now democracy must be preserved'","url":"https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2024-election/live-blog/dnc-2024-live-updates-rcna165226#rcrd52632","datePublished":"2024-08-20T03:44:59.643Z","dateModified":"2024-08-20T03:47:51.413Z","author":[{"@type":"Person","name":"Rebecca Shabad","image":"https://media-cldnry.s-nbcnews.com/image/upload/t_fit-1500w,q_auto:best/newscms/2023_08/3595832/rebecca-shabad-byline-jm-1.jpg","jobTitle":"Politics Reporter","description":"Rebecca Shabad is a politics reporter for NBC News based in Washington.","disambiguatingDescription":"Before NBC News, I was a political reporter at CBS News, covering national politics and policy for its website during the 2016 presidential election cycle and the first year of Trumpâs presidency. I wrote about how Russian hackers could disrupt the U.S. election, issues surrounding voting machines and the Trump administrationâs vetting process for refugees. I also wrote an exclusive story that went viral about sleeveless dresses being banned in the House, which prompted a change in the dress code. At The Hill newspaper, I reported on the federal budget process, government spending as well as on breaking political news. I got my start in journalism at CQ Roll Call. Iâm a graduate of Syracuse Universityâs S.I. Newhouse School of Public Communications, with a degree in broadcast journalism and political science.","url":"https://www.nbcnews.com/author/rebecca-shabad-ncpn854016","sameAs":["https://x.com/rebeccashabad"]}],"articleBody":"Biden said he wants to report that \"democracy has prevailed, democracy has delivered, and now democracy must be preserved.\"\n\n\"We're facing an inflection point, one of those rare moments in history when the decisions we make now will determine the fate of our nation and the world for decades to come. That's not hyperbole. I mean it literally. We're in a battle for the very soul of America,\" he said. \n\nHe spoke about what occurred in Charlottesville, Virginia, in 2017 and how it motivated him to run for president again. \n\n\"I ran for president in 2020 because of what I saw in Charlottesville,\" he said. \"Extremists coming out of the woods, carrying torches, their veins bulging from their necks, carrying Nazi swastikas, chanting the same exact antisemitic bile that was heard in Germany in the early â30s, neo-Nazis, white supremacists, the Ku Klux Klan, so emboldened by a president then in the White House that they saw as an ally [that] they didn't even bother to wear their hoods.\" ","image":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://media-cldnry.s-nbcnews.com/image/upload/t_fit-1500w,q_auto:best/rockcms/2024-08/240816-dnc-liveblog-jg-d2a0f2.jpg","caption":""}},{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"BlogPosting","publisher":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"NBC Gaza. Other attendees tried to block the flag with âWe â¤ï¸ Joeâ signs before one snatched the flag and threw it aside. Chants of âWe love Joeâ also drowned out attempts by the protesters to interrupt Bidenâs remarks.\n\nThe commotion went largely unnoticed by Biden or most of the people in the convention center.","image":{"@type":"ImageObject","url":"https://media-cldnry.s-nbcnews.com/image/upload/t_fit-1500w,q_auto:best/rockcms/2024-08/240816-dnc-liveblog-jg-d2a0f2.jpg","caption":""}},{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"BlogPosting","publisher":{"@type":"NewsMediaOrganization","name":"NBC
